framework.name: HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164, Subpart C) + Privacy Rule minimum-necessary
framework.citation: 45 CFR Part 164 Subpart C (Security Rule); 164.502(b) is Subpart E (Privacy Rule); PHI identifiers per Safe Harbor 164.514(b)(2)
framework.note: A convenience index, NOT a compliance claim or legal advice. Ballast can only observe what flows through it — model exchanges and tool actions — so this pack covers the handful of HIPAA provisions that are runtime-observable and leaves the ~80% that are administrative, physical, and organizational process (risk analysis, workforce training, facility access, contingency planning, BA contracts, etc.) to your broader program. It keeps Ballast's normal detection (extends_default) and adds matchers: when clinical/PHI context, a plaintext transmission, or incident language appears, Ballast flags the record and tags it to the relevant provision so an auditor can pull it. Matchers are keyword/regex heuristics based on the Safe Harbor identifiers (164.514(b)(2)); expect false positives — a starting point, not clinical NLP. NOTE: 164.502(b) minimum-necessary is a Privacy Rule provision, included because PHI-presence is what Ballast can detect. Integrity (164.312(c)) is provided by the tamper-evident hash-chain — the real evidence is `ballast verify`, not the per-record tag. IMPORTANT: an audit trail of a system handling ePHI is itself ePHI and must be protected accordingly (redaction is on by default and this pack adds MRN redaction, but the trail is in scope). Not a full HIPAA solution. Revise for your own context.

controls.title: Audit controls
controls.summary: Implement mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI.
controls.relevance: Ballast records and lets you examine every model exchange and action; this control covers all activity (ambient).

controls.title: Information system activity review
controls.summary: Regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs and access/incident reports.
controls.relevance: Ballast's trail is the activity record to be reviewed; `ballast log` / `summary` are the review surface. Covers all activity (ambient).

controls.title: Integrity
controls.summary: Protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction, with a mechanism to corroborate it has not been altered.
controls.relevance: Every record is hash-chained (and optionally HMAC-sealed). The evidence is the chain itself — verify it with `ballast verify`; the per-record tag only marks that the record participates in it.

controls.title: Minimum necessary (PHI present) — Privacy Rule
controls.summary: Uses and disclosures of PHI must be limited to the minimum necessary. PHI combines health information with the identifiers at 164.514(b)(2) (names, dates, MRN, health-plan/account numbers, contacts).
controls.relevance: When clinical or PHI context appears in the exchange or a tool action, Ballast flags and tags the record so an auditor can review whether only the minimum-necessary PHI was used. Common identifiers are redacted in storage.

controls.title: Transmission security
controls.summary: Guard against unauthorized access to ePHI transmitted over a network; implementation specs call for integrity controls and encryption.
controls.relevance: Fires only when PHI context AND a plaintext (non-TLS) endpoint appear in the same action — a possible unencrypted transmission of ePHI. Flags and tags it for review. Heuristic: it cannot confirm the payload was ePHI, only that both signals co-occur.

controls.title: Security incident procedures (response and reporting)
controls.summary: Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents, mitigate their effects, and document them.
controls.relevance: When the exchange or an action contains language suggesting a security incident (breach, unauthorized access, exfiltration), Ballast flags and tags the record for incident review.
